title: The Constitutional Agency Allocator: one allocator that compiles all three telemetry laws into hard gates, tested on real GitHub + Hugging Face (pre-registered BEFORE running)
terminology_note: This spec uses ONLY the functional, engineering meanings of the governance primitives (encoding fidelity, decoding fidelity, systemic yield, two-source independence, enforcement latency). No cultural or religious lexicon is used, by request, to keep the artifact bias-free and substrate-independent.
thesis: The prior triage-first allocator (PR #105) used only Law 1's collapse floor. This upgrades it into a single Constitutional Agency Allocator that compiles ALL THREE validated telemetry laws into active, call-time gates, and asks whether the upgraded allocator is measurably better and un-gameable on REAL open-source substrates (GitHub, Hugging Face). Because LISM prioritizes nulls, any gate that adds no value on the real data is reported as such, not retuned.

laws_recap.law_1_linear_coupling: E = U * D_enc * D_dec  -- systemic yield is the TWO-HOP product of capacity and both fidelity legs; either leg at zero zeroes the term.
laws_recap.law_1_independence: a viable node state requires a TWO-SOURCE independent join: the two fidelity legs must be independent measurements, not one source re-certifying itself (anti-hoarding / variance-inflation gate).
laws_recap.law_2_enforcement_latency: tau_v -- a node's unresolved backlog (enforcement latency) is the continuous early-warning compass; rising latency shrinks the node's query radius (its authority to absorb shared budget).
laws_recap.law_3_decoupled_evaluation: F_out = F_eval -- verdicts are computed from measured, held-out telemetry, blind to any self-certified claim; dF_out/dF_gen = 0 (proven in PR #106).

substrates.github: 28 real most-starred repos (frozen github-lism fixture). U = log10(stars). enc = forks/(forks+open_issues) [backlog-adjusted adoption -- the reference-lock leg]. dec = clip(forks/stars,0,1) [fork-through / propagation efficiency -- the peer-propagation leg]. tau_v proxy = open_issues (unresolved backlog, per the fixture provenance). floor on min(enc,dec) = 0.30.
substrates.huggingface: 19 real media-gen models (frozen hf-media fixture). U = log10(1+downloads). enc = license clarity in {0.9 permissive, 0.5 restrictive, 0.25 unknown}. dec = 0.3 + 0.6*[has eval_results]. The two legs come from DISTINCT real fields (license text vs benchmark evidence) -> naturally independent. floor on min(enc,dec) = 0.28.

allocator: Constitutional allocator distributes a fixed integer budget B = 3 * (#below-floor). At each step it picks the (node, leg) with the highest TRUE two-hop marginal value dE/dinvest = U * (other leg) * absorption(node), rescues below-floor legs first (their unlock jump U*floor*other dominates an incremental U*step*other), credits a node's two-hop product ONLY if it passes the independence gate, and discounts the gain by the node's enforcement-latency absorption. Baselines: capacity-greedy (max U), equal-split, and the prior triage allocator (Law-1-only).

gates.G1_two_hop_objective: LAW 1. The Constitutional allocator's realized systemic yield E >= capacity-greedy AND >= equal-split on BOTH substrates, AND >= the prior triage allocator on both; strictly greater than capacity-greedy on at least one real substrate. PASS iff so.
gates.G2_independence_gate: LAW 1 (join). Deterministic anti-hoarding control: an injected self-certifying 'hoarder' node whose decoding leg is a byte-copy of its encoding leg (single source, VIF -> infinity) is FLAGGED and its two-hop credit VOIDED by the independence gate, while every real (distinct-leg) node PASSES. PASS iff the hoarder is rejected and all real nodes pass. Descriptive (not thresholded, reported honestly): the measured cohort VIF(enc,dec) for GitHub and HF.
gates.G3_tau_v_throttle: LAW 2. Using real unresolved-backlog as the tau_v proxy and a committed absorption model absorption = 1/(1 + tau_v / tau_ref) with tau_ref = median backlog, the tau_v-AWARE Constitutional allocator's realized E (scored under the true absorption dynamics) is compared to the tau_v-BLIND prior allocator. Report dE = E_aware - E_blind. FALSIFIABLE: dE >= 0 is the predicted improvement; dE ~ 0 is an honest 'the throttle adds no value on this cohort' null; dE < 0 would be a FAIL reported not hidden.
gates.G4_decoupled_shield: LAW 3. The full allocation vector and final E are INVARIANT to nodes' self-certified claims: perturbing a claimed_fidelity field over {honest, 1e3, 1e6, 1e9} leaves the allocation byte-identical -> variance == 0 == dF_out/dF_gen. PASS iff variance == 0. (Inherits the PR #106 capstone.)

symmetric_null: Every substrate's below-floor count, each allocator's realized E, dE for the throttle, the measured VIFs, and the decoupling variance are reported AS-IS. G1 and G4 are structural (a marginal-value greedy dominates capacity-greedy; decoupling is by construction) -- if either fails it is a critical bug, reported. G3 is genuinely empirical and MAY be a null; that null is a valid, reported outcome, never retuned into a win. If a substrate has zero below-floor nodes, triage is declared not-applicable (a survivor null), not forced.
acceptance: GREEN when the spec re-hashes to the manifest, the two fixtures re-hash to their pins, G1/G2/G4 hold, and G3 is reported honestly (dE >= 0, or an explicitly-declared null). No post-hoc gate changes.
honest_scope: This is a governance METHODOLOGY, not a speed or superhuman-intelligence claim. It shows that compiling the three laws into one allocator is (a) never worse and sometimes better than naive allocation on real substrates, (b) immune to self-certification and forged claims, and (c) responsive to enforcement latency -- or honestly null where the real data gives no lever. It does not claim to allocate optimally against an unknown ground truth; it claims to be an active, verifiable, un-gameable method. Complements PR #104 (discovery), #105 (triage substrates), #106 (decoupling capstone).
